pi_items.source: export_result.sh and verifier.py logic explicitly use this exact path for output validation.
export_result.sh and verifier.py logic explicitly require this path to read the max calibration altitude.
export_result.sh and verifier.py logic explicitly require this path to verify the prediction apogee exceeds 550m.
export_result.sh and verifier.py logic require this path for technical content verification.
setup_task.sh copies /workspace/data/rockets/dual_parachute_deployment.ork, which is an authentic OpenRocket example by Joe Pfeiffer.
verifier.py checks if the max altitude in the calibration CSV is within tolerance of 415m.
verifier.py uses this explicit 10m tolerance against the absolute difference from the target apogee.
verifier.py mandates a score >= 65 along with the anti-gaming and calibration apogee conditions to pass.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real OpenRocket example model ("Dual parachute deployment.ork" by Joe Pfeiffer). The target apogee (415m) and tolerance (10m) explicitly correspond to the scoring criteria in verifier.py, which also strictly checks that no artificial mass components or altered launch conditions were introduced to cheat the calibration. The output file paths for the calibrated .ork, CSV exports, and report exactly match the export and verification scripts.
